text: Since it's self adherence and adherence to plastic is so good, I bet you could add a tiny part of micro glass bead and make a formulation that's unbelievably abrasion resistant without ruining the conductivity. Not saying it needs such a modification, just throwing it out there.

author: @scotttovey
text:
Scrubbing under water doesn't take it off.
You had to take a layer of plastic off to get it off.
If you need to adhere two pieces of plastic together, you don't need to purchase a different product, just use your conductive ink.
In fact, this will allow you to sandwich a circuit between two pieces of plastic, but you need to think ahead to insure you have something to connect external wiring too.

replies.author: @GofuKyersen
replies.text: And stack them if you have connections between, either up the sides or holes punched through at appropriate places.

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text:
@GofuKyersen 
Yeah, if you can make logic gates in the circuitry; as in a micro processor, you could build a computer processor or microprocessor. It wouldn't be as powerful or as functional, but it would be a cool computer science tutorial and object demo that you can show kids.
